raw_consumer_statement:
Old Navy online order XXXX XXXXXXXX placed on XX/XX/2024. 

I ordered 9 items, the online tracking showing all of the times were delivered in 1 package. However, I received an XL size empty bag with only 2 small items inside. The package looked like it had been opened previously and re-taped with clear packaging tape. I filed a claim with Old Navy regarding 7 missing items asking for a refund. 

Additionally, I redeemed my Cash rewards dollars, XXXX XXXX and otherwise utilized a total of {$76.00} in multiple promotions in this order. Since 7 out of 9 items I ordered were never delivered, I had to re-order those items at a full price, which cost me 2-3 times more than the original cost, so I asked to be reimbursed for the difference as well. 

On XX/XX/2024 I received a response from Old Navy customer service saying that my request for a refund had been denied, without any reason or further explanation. I was completely dumbfounded. I was a loyal customer for many years, and buy A LOT from this company on a regular basis. In all my life I've never had to deal with a retail company refusing to refund merchandize I paid for, but they failed to deliver. They can't do that, can they??
